message.content:
POST-MORTEM. What went wrong (measured, on the real 329-basket corpus): S2/S3 produced a cp3 corpus
where only 3/995 evidence rows had a DOI and 3/995 had a journal name, and 253/995 were tier=UNKNOWN
— even though the metadata was PLAINLY PRESENT in each row's own direct_quote text and source_url
(e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo's row literally contains "Journal of Economic Perspectives" and DOI
10.1257/jep.33.2.3, yet its doi/journal fields were blank). A trivial downstream deterministic pass
(regex DOI + domain->venue map) recovered doi 3->240, journal 3->294, and cut UNKNOWN 253->145.
So S2/S3 SILENTLY DROPPED metadata it had in hand. Consequences: good peer-reviewed sources got
mislabeled UNKNOWN (looks low-quality), instruction-following on journal-demanding tasks tanked, and
nothing anywhere FAILED or warned — the corpus looked fine.

READ-ONLY: read the S2/S3 code but DO NOT WRITE to /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (a fix wheel is editing it).
Read from /workspace/POLARIS/src (or the s2s3 worktree) read-only; write any findings to
/home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md ONLY. The proven recovery logic is at
/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py. Corpus: cp3_basket_snapshot.json
(995 rows). Be concrete: file:line for the failure. Honest — if the extractor was never written vs
written-but-never-called vs silently-excepted, say which.


YOU ARE FABLE. Judge the post-mortem.
ROOT CAUSE: {
  "mechanism": "The DOI/journal/tier extractor for the S2/S3 path was NEVER WRITTEN. S2 (scripts/s2_select_replay.py HEAD) builds the cp2 kept-row pool with zero metadata enrichment: pass-through rows are appended raw (line 252 `kept_rows.append(row)`) and screened rows go through `ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res)` (line 268), which is defined in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386 and only rewrites the body field + attaches a line_screen sidecar — it does `dict(row)` (preserving any doi/journal already present) but never parses direct_quote/source_url to derive doi/journal/tier. There is no import of any extractor at HEAD. S3 (scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py HEAD) then only reads the fields S2 never filled: `_tier(i)` at line 101-102 returns `str(rows[i].get('tier','') or 'UNKNOWN')` — a wrong-default that silently coerces every blank tier to 'UNKNOWN' (that single `or 'UNKNOWN'` is the 253 UNKNOWN) — and there is no `_doi`/`_journal` accessor and no member_dois/member_journals at HEAD at all, so DOI/journal were never even surfaced. The DOI in source_url (e.g. .../doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3) and the 'Journal of Economic Perspectives' string in direct_quote were in hand on every row and never read. The ~5 rows that arrived pre-populated upstream (acemoglu) flowed through intact, proving it is absence-of-extraction, not a transform drop.",
  "file_line": "scripts/s2_select_replay.py:248-268 (no enrichment; apply_result_to_row at src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386) and scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:101-102 (_tier ... or 'UNKNOWN'; no _doi/_journal)",
  "failure_class": "never_written",
  "mechanism_note": "n/a",
  "was_silent": true,
  "evidence": "Confirmed against committed HEAD of /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (working tree is mid-fix by the wheel: it adds untracked src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py + enrich_row_metadata calls — the fix, not the bug). HEAD s2_select_replay.py has no metadata extractor import and no doi/journal/tier writes; apply_result_to_row (line_screen.py:1361-1386) touches only body+sidecar. HEAD s3_consolidate_replay.py grep shows only `def _tier` (101) and `member_tiers_weight` (177) — no _doi/_journal/member_dois. The shipped outputs prove silence: outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json has no metadata_enrichment_summary; outputs/s2s3_repass/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json has no corpus_metadata_summary and baskets lack member_dois. Measured on that cp2 (728 rows): only 5 doi, only 5 journal, 177 UNKNOWN; 723 blank-doi rows, 156 with a DOI directly regex-recoverable from their own source_url/direct_quote (21 from url, 135 from quote); blank rows (e.g. ev_454) don't even carry a doi/journal key. Nothing raised or warned; stage summaries never measured population. Full write-up: /home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md",
  "other_silent_losses": [
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:102 _tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN' — wrong-default silently laundering any missing tier into UNKNOWN",
    "scripts/s2_select_replay.py ~line 221 topic-judge except Exception (fail-open) — any judge error silently yields zero off-subject stamps",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 142 _stmt bare except around extract_numeric_claims — swallows extraction errors, silent fallback to weaker representative",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 63 _nli_score_stats except Exception — semantic-merge telemetry can silently become unavailable",
    "scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 351 unified_deletion_disclosure except Exception — swallows S2 summary.json read/parse errors",
    "Row schema by omission: blank rows (e.g. ev_454) carry no doi/journal key at all; every .get('doi','') consumer sees empty and no S2/S3 code backfills, so the gap is invisible without a population lock-bar that HEAD never had"
  ]
}
PREVENTION: {
  "invariant": "Add assert_metadata_invariants(rows, stage) to src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py: a per-row STRUCTURAL RESIDUAL check (not a population threshold). R1: if DOI_RE.search(direct_quote OR source_url) matches, doi MUST be non-empty. R2: if domain_of(url) is in DOMAIN_TIER (known scholarly/gov/venue set), tier MUST NOT be UNKNOWN/blank. On any violation it raises SystemExit with the offending counts and example evidence_ids (e.g. 'rows with a DOI in their own quote/url but doi BLANK: 156', 'rows on a known-journal domain but tier=UNKNOWN: 47'). Because the guard recomputes with the SAME DOI_RE/DOMAIN_TIER the extractor uses, extractor and check cannot disagree — a non-zero residual is a proof the extractor did not run on those rows, i.e. the exact HEAD bug. Measured: fires on every bug-state cp2 (140-240 DOI + 44-52 tier residuals -> hard fail); reads 0/0 on the enriched cp2 (s2s3_meta). The existing wheel fix only MEASURES population (s2_select_replay.py:275-299, s3_consolidate_replay.py:223-251) with no assert/raise — this converts measurement into a fail-loud gate. A threshold on doi_populated cannot be used because population is query-dependent; only the per-row residual is safe.",
  "where_it_runs": "Both stage write-sites so it fires on every corpus build. (1) S2 scripts/s2_select_replay.py right after the lock-bar block (after line 293, before cp2['evidence_for_gen']=kept_rows at ~297): cp2['metadata_invariant']=assert_metadata_invariants(kept_rows, stage='s2') — gates cp2 at the disk write, and kept_rows includes the pass-through branch that was the original blank-row source. (2) S3 scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py right after corpus_metadata_summary is built (after line 251): assert_metadata_invariants(rows, stage='s3') over the full cp2 pool S3 consumes — catches a cp2 produced by an old/rogue pre-guard S2 and refuses to consolidate rather than laundering blanks into UNKNOWN at _tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN' (s3:112-113).",
  "is_query_agnostic": true,
  "false_positive_risk": "Bounded and measured to zero on this corpus. R1 fires ONLY when a DOI is literally present in the row's own text/url, so genuine non-journal sources with no DOI (blogs, press releases, gov landing pages) never trip — the 480-754 legitimately DOI-less rows per corpus all pass (0 false positives confirmed across 7 corpora). R2 is a whitelist: a finance/medical domain not yet in DOMAIN_TIER is simply not checked by R2 (still fully protected by the universal R1), so unknown domains cannot false-fire. Residual R2 edge case: a known-journal domain whose specific URL is a non-article page (publisher About page) would be tiered by publisher tier — acceptable and deterministic, so check and extractor still agree. Extractor and check share DOI_RE/DOMAIN_TIER, eliminating any independent-regex drift class of false positive.",
  "test": "tests/test_s2s3_metadata_invariant.py with three cases. test_head_bug_state_fails_loud_without_enrichment builds the EXACT HEAD failure (Acemoglu row: DOI in direct_quote, JEP on aeaweb.org, but doi/journal blank and tier absent; plus a medical NEJM DOI row on pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov in the same blank state; plus a legit DOI-less blog) and asserts assert_metadata_invariants raises SystemExit naming 'doi BLANK: 2' and 'tier=UNKNOWN: 2' (blog excluded) — this is red at HEAD, catching the bug. test_enrichment_then_invariant_passes runs enrich_row_metadata then the invariant, asserts 0/0 residuals and that rows[0].doi=='10.1257/jep.33.2.3', journal=='Journal of Economic Perspectives', and the medical rows[1].doi=='10.1056/NEJMoa2035389' (proving query-agnostic recovery) while the blog stays doi==''. test_blog_never_false_positives locks the false-positive boundary. The check being structural (DOI regex + domain class, no topic keywords) means it keeps catching this class on any future query, finance or medical or otherwise."
}
Answer: (1) Is the root cause the REAL mechanism (file:line verified), or a plausible guess? (2) Is the
prevention GENUINELY query-agnostic and fail-loud — would it catch this on a finance/medical corpus
too, and would it have failed the original bad run instead of passing it silently? (3) Is there a
BROADER lesson — is silent data-loss-without-failure a pattern elsewhere in the pipeline (the corpus
'looked fine' while being broken)? (4) The single most important guard to add so 'looks fine but is
silently broken' cannot ship again. Be blunt; the operator called this a fatal, stupid mistake and
wants it structurally impossible to repeat.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

    )

    # Build cp2: kept rows (whole-drops removed) with kept-lines-only bodies + sidecar.
    result_by_id = {r.evidence_id: r for r in corpus.results}
    kept_rows: list[dict] = []
    dropped_line_records: list[dict] = []
    whole_drop_records: list[dict] = []
    for row in rows:
        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
        res = result_by_id.get(eid)
        if res is None:
            kept_rows.append(row)
            continue
        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("statement") or "")
        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
        for d in res.dropped:
            dropped_line_records.append({
                "evidence_id": eid, "title": title, "url": url,
                "line_idx": d.get("line_idx"), "reason": d.get("reason"),
                "quote": d.get("quote", ""),
            })
        if res.whole_dropped:
            whole_drop_records.append({
                "evidence_id": eid, "title": title, "url": url,
                "reason": res.whole_drop_reason, "n_lines": res.n_lines,
            })
            continue  # excluded from the grounding pool (disclosed)
        kept_rows.append(ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res))

    # ── cp2_corpus_snapshot.json ──
    cp2 = dict(data)
    cp2["evidence_for_gen"] = kept_rows
    cp2["stage"] = "s2_line_screened"
    cp2["line_screen_summary"] = {
        "n_sources_in": len(rows),
        "n_sources_kept": len(kept_rows),
        "n_whole_dropped": len(whole_drop_records),
        "n_dropped_lines": len(dropped_line_records),
        "scope_armed": scope.armed,
        "scope_active": scope.is_active(),
        # §-1.3.1 fail-loud DISCLOSURE (S2/S3 re-pass iter-6): count of rows the meaning-level
        # topic judge CONFIDENTLY stamped OFF_SUBJECT that nonetheless SURVIVED into cp2. These
        # are the disclosed FAIL-OPEN residual: the OFF_TOPIC whole-drop is a TWO-KEY concurrence
        # (topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT stamp + line-screen 100%-off_topic) precisely so a single-pass
        # judge FALSE-POSITIVE on a credible ON-TOPIC source is NEVER deleted (a drb_72 example is
        # the Roosevelt-Institute "Good Life Agenda", which explicitly discusses AI's labor impact
        # yet was stamped OFF_SUBJECT; the two-key gate correctly KEEPS it). Surfacing the count
        # makes the residual AUDITABLE instead of silent; these rows stay DEMOTED (weight, not
        # filter) and flow to composition where the faithfulness engine is the only hard gate.
        "n_offsubject_stamped_kept": sum(
            1 for r in kept_rows if r.get("topic_off_subject") is True
        ),
    }
    (out_dir / "cp2_corpus_snapshot.json").write_text(
        json.dumps(cp2, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    # ── disclosure.txt (every dropped line quoted) ──
    lines_out: list[str] = []
    lines_out.append("S2 LINE-SCREEN DISCLOSURE — every dropped line quoted (§-1.3.1 fail-loud)")
    lines_out.append(f"snapshot: {snap_path}")

def apply_result_to_row(row: dict[str, Any], result: SourceScreenResult) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Return a COPY of ``row`` with the grounding body rewritten to the KEPT lines only (V4)
    + a ``line_screen`` sidecar. The original body is left intact in the caller's audit copy
    (fetch_snapshot). A whole-dropped source is NOT returned by the caller's kept pool — this
    still rewrites its body (empty) for a consistent record. Pure (never mutates the input)."""
    new_row = dict(row)
    kept = result.kept_body
    # Rewrite the field that held the widest body (the grounding surface composition cites).
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            _CHROME_BODY_FIELDS,
        )
        fields = _CHROME_BODY_FIELDS
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
        fields = ("direct_quote", "statement")
    widest_key = max(
        (k for k in fields if isinstance(row.get(k), str) and row.get(k)),
        key=lambda k: len(str(row.get(k))), default="direct_quote",
    )
    new_row[widest_key] = kept
    if "direct_quote" in new_row and widest_key != "direct_quote":
        # keep direct_quote consistent with the screened body when it WAS the cite surface
        if isinstance(row.get("direct_quote"), str) and row.get("direct_quote"):
            new_row["direct_quote"] = kept
    new_row["line_screen"] = result.sidecar()
    return new_row

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, re
DOI_RE = re.compile(r'10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s"<>]+')
for p in ["outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json","outputs/s2s3_repass/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json"]:
    try:
        d=json.load(open(p))
    except Exception as e:
        print(p,"ERR",e); continue
    rows = d.get("evidence_for_gen") or d.get("rows") or []
    if not rows and "baskets" in d:
        print(p,"keys:",list(d.keys())[:12], "n_baskets:", len(d["baskets"]))
        continue
    ndoi=sum(1 for r in rows if r.get("doi"))
    njour=sum(1 for r in rows if r.get("journal"))
    nunk=sum(1 for r in rows if str(r.get("tier","") or "UNKNOWN")=="UNKNOWN")
    recover=sum(1 for r in rows if not r.get("doi") and (DOI_RE.search(str(r.get("source_url","")))or DOI_RE.search(str(r.get("direct_quote","")))))
    print(p, "rows:",len(rows), "doi:",ndoi,"journal:",njour,"UNKNOWN:",nunk,"regex-recoverable-doi:",recover)
    print("  has metadata_enrichment_summary:", "metadata_enrichment_summary" in d, "| corpus_metadata_summary:", "corpus_metadata_summary" in d)
EOF

import json

def walk(o):
    if isinstance(o,dict):
        for k,v in o.items():
            if k in ('evidence_for_gen','evidence','rows') and isinstance(v,list): return v
            r=walk(v)
            if r: return r
    return None

EOF

content.text:
VERDICT: The post-mortem is accurate. I independently re-verified every load-bearing claim against committed HEAD (7b72d41) and the shipped artifacts. Detail per question:

(1) ROOT CAUSE — REAL, verified, not a guess.
- `git show HEAD:scripts/s2_select_replay.py`: pass-through rows appended raw at line 252 (`kept_rows.append(row)`), screened rows via `ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res)` at line 268. Zero grep hits for doi/journal/enrich/source_metadata anywhere in HEAD S2. Confirmed.
- `apply_result_to_row` is at HEAD `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386` exactly as claimed: `dict(row)` copy, rewrites the widest body field + attaches `line_screen` sidecar, derives nothing. Confirmed.
- HEAD `scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:101-102`: `_tier` with `or 'UNKNOWN'`; HEAD has no `_doi`/`_journal`/`member_dois` (only `_tier`:101 and `member_tiers_weight`:177). The working tree now has `_doi` (~line 115) and `member_dois`/`member_journals` (198-199) — that is the wheel's in-progress fix, correctly distinguished from the bug. Confirmed.
- Failure class `never_written` is the honest call: no extractor existed at HEAD to be uncalled or to except. `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py` is untracked (new), i.e. the fix.
- Measurements reproduced exactly: shipped `outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json` = 728 rows, 5 doi, 5 journal, 177 UNKNOWN, 156 regex-recoverable DOIs; no `metadata_enrichment_summary`. Shipped iter7 cp3 (329 baskets, 385 members, 95 UNKNOWN) has no `member_dois` and no `corpus_metadata_summary`. The repair script header independently corroborates 992/995 blank, 253 UNKNOWN on the outline-agent corpus. `was_silent: true` is correct — nothing in HEAD measures field population, only row counts.
- All six "other silent losses" check out at HEAD: s2:221 fail-open judge except, s3:63, s3:153 (claimed ~142), s3:362 (claimed ~351) — two line numbers drift by ~10 but are flagged as approximate.

(2) PREVENTION — genuinely query-agnostic and fail-loud AS DESIGNED, with one material caveat: IT IS NOT IMPLEMENTED YET. `assert_metadata_invariants` exists nowhere in the tree, and `tests/test_s2s3_metadata_invariant.py` does not exist — the prevention text reads as shipped but is a spec. That said, I reproduced its claimed measurements myself using the wheel's own `DOI_RE`/`DOMAIN_TIER`: on the bug-state cp2, R1 residual = 156, R2 residual = 47 (inside the claimed 140-240 / 44-52 ranges) — so the guard WOULD have hard-failed the original run instead of passing it. It is query-agnostic: R1 is pure structure (a DOI in the row's own bytes → doi must be populated), which fires identically on a finance (10.1111/jofi...) or medical (10.1056/NEJMoa...) corpus; R2 is whitelist-bounded so unknown domains cannot false-fire. The shared-DOI_RE design eliminates check/extractor drift — the residual is a proof the extractor didn't run. Two gaps worth naming: (a) sharing DOI_RE also shares blind spots (a too-narrow regex passes both sides silently — acceptable, since R1 still catches "extractor never ran", the actual bug class); (b) the invariant guards doi and tier but NOT journal population directly, and journal (3→294 recovered) was a large part of the loss — a known-journal-domain-but-journal-blank residual should be added as R3. The critique of the wheel fix is correct and important: I verified the current working-tree diff only computes and prints summaries — no assert, no raise. As it stands, a future enrichment regression would ship silently again.

(3) BROADER LESSON — yes, this is a pattern, verified in-tree. Four instances of the same shape at HEAD: fail-open `except Exception` on the topic judge (s2:221 — a judge outage yields zero off-subject stamps, indistinguishable from a clean corpus), swallowed `extract_numeric_claims` errors (s3:153), swallowed NLI telemetry (s3:63), swallowed S2-summary read errors (s3:362), plus two non-except variants: the wrong-default `or 'UNKNOWN'` that launders absence into a legitimate-looking category, and schema-by-omission (blank rows carry no doi/journal key at all, so every `.get('doi','')` consumer sees a valid-looking empty string). The unifying defect: stage summaries measure FLOW (n_sources_kept, n_dropped) but never CONTENT (field population), so a corpus can be structurally intact and semantically gutted while every gauge reads green.

(4) SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT GUARD — the per-row structural-residual invariant at every snapshot WRITE site, fail-closed: no stage may serialize a corpus containing a row whose own bytes prove a derivable field is blank. This is the right single guard because it is self-proving (recomputed from the row itself, no threshold, no query dependence) and it sits at the only chokepoint every corpus must pass (the disk write). Concretely: land `assert_metadata_invariants` exactly as specced — S2 after the lock-bar before the cp2 write, S3 over the consumed pool before consolidation — and add the R3 journal residual. The generalization the operator should demand: every "summary" block any stage writes must be an asserted contract, not telemetry; and the fail-open `except Exception` sites must each write a counted, disclosed residual that a downstream gate checks is zero or explained. Priority order: (1) commit the invariant + red-at-HEAD test (currently vaporware), (2) convert the wheel's print-only lock-bars into raises, (3) sweep the four except sites.

Files: /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (HEAD 7b72d41, read-only), /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json, /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/outputs/s2s3_repass/iter7/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json, /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py, /home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md.
